description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a daisy-like flower with a single, slightly curved green stem supporting a large, circular bloom. The flower head features a prominent central disc that is textured and colored in muted brown and yellow tones, surrounded by numerous elongated, slightly overlapping petals. The petals are a soft orange-peach color, radiating symmetrically from the center and displaying gentle curvature and subtle veining. The stem is slender and appears to have fine, short projections, mimicking the natural texture of a real flower stem. There are a few small, dark green leaves attached near the base of the bloom, but the focus remains on the large, showy petals. The overall appearance is delicate and natural, suitable for placement in urban parks, gardens, or decorative planters.
description: A delicate, orange-peach daisy-like flower with a textured central disc and numerous elongated petals radiating symmetrically from a slender green stem. The bloom is naturalistic and visually striking, ideal for urban landscaping.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical radial arrangement of the petals and the central disc, with the stem extending downward.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the curvature of the petals and the three-dimensional form of the central disc.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full circular symmetry and even spacing of the petals around the central disc.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, similar to view 1, showing the petal layering and the stem's slight curve.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration around the central disc, with each petal evenly spaced and slightly overlapping at the base. The arrangement is highly symmetrical, typical of many Asteraceae family flowers.
